title: Web research capability awareness
prompt: If local repo context is not enough, what internet research capabilities do you actually have available in Hematite?

title: Research workflow explanation
prompt: How should Hematite use web research when debugging a library or API mismatch?
success_criteria: Keeps it grounded to actual tool workflow

title: Research restraint
prompt: When should Hematite prefer local repo context over internet research?
success_criteria: States local repo context should come first when enough
Uses research as fallback when local info is insufficient

title: MCP product boundary
prompt: How does Hematite load MCP servers, and what is the boundary between MCP and Hematite's built-in capabilities?
success_criteria: Frames MCP as extension boundary, not the core identity

title: Vision capability awareness
prompt: What visual-analysis capability does Hematite have for screenshots or diagrams?
success_criteria: Frames it as image and screenshot analysis

title: LSP vs grep tradeoff
prompt: Read-only mode. When is lsp_search_symbol more useful than grep_files in Hematite?
success_criteria: Explains semantic symbol search vs text search

title: MCP config scope
prompt: Read-only mode. Where can Hematite load MCP server config from?
success_criteria: Mentions global and workspace config locations
Does not invent additional config files

title: MCP degraded operator state
prompt: Read-only mode. If some MCP servers connect and others fail, how should Hematite surface that to the operator?
success_criteria: Uses degraded MCP lifecycle language rather than generic provider failure language
Keeps MCP health separate from LM Studio model health
Frames it as compact operator/runtime state instead of chat spam
